coverage_heuristic.description: Automated planning heuristic. n = questions assessed, s = distinct subskills. n=0 -> not_assessed; n=1 -> limited; n 2-3 -> moderate unless all questions test one subskill (then limited); n>=4 -> strong if s>=2 else moderate.

weak_cluster_rule.description: A subskill is a weak cluster when at least 2 assessed questions test it and its raw accuracy is below 60%, or when a teacher note explicitly flags the subskill. A single isolated wrong answer is not a cluster unless flagged as foundational.

timing_adjustments.note: Expected-time defaults are documented planning assumptions used only when a diagnostic template does not define per-question expected times. slow = time > 1.5x expected max; rushed = wrong and time < 0.5x expected min. severe pacing = at least half of assessed questions in the lesson slow or rushed (min 3 questions).

high_target_adjustment.conditions: Applied only when target >= 1400 and distinct advanced-level evidence exists: an incorrect hard question, a slow hard question, a teacher-flagged advanced trap/careless pattern, or an explicit advanced_forms_unverified flag. Limited or absent coverage alone does not trigger this addition.

rounding.lesson_rounding: none; retain two-decimal calculated lesson allocations
rounding.group_lesson_rounding: none; retain two-decimal calculated group allocations
rounding.policy: Only the final individual or group live-hour total is rounded upward to the next quarter hour. Exact quarter-hour totals remain unchanged.

broad_lesson_half_hour_conditions: Not applicable. Broad and non-broad lessons share the same absolute 0.25-hour minimum.

individual_course_limits.minimum_rationale: 14 hours is the configured program minimum. It is an operational floor, not a mathematical consequence of lesson minima.
individual_course_limits.maximum_rationale: 35 hours is a strict operational live-course maximum enforced by changing actual lesson and strategy components, never by a headline-only clamp.

priority_rules.critical: weighted accuracy < 40, or a major foundational gap
priority_rules.high: weighted accuracy 40-59.99, a moderate foundational gap, or 2+ weak subskill clusters
priority_rules.medium: weighted accuracy 60-89.99, or lesson not assessed / limited coverage
priority_rules.essential_review: weighted accuracy >= 90 with at least moderate coverage

group_split_triggers: raw total exceeds the group maximum
at least 25% of lessons are highly different
highest and lowest individual course totals differ by more than 35%
one subset needs foundational instruction while another needs only review
more than 8 students with significant diversity
more than 12 students with a high-personalization promise
incompatible target scores or test dates

course_constraint_allocation.exact_remainder_policy: After quarter-hour blocks, an exact hundredth-hour remainder may be assigned or removed so the actual component sum reaches the 14-hour floor or 35-hour ceiling. Individual lesson calculations themselves are not rounded.
individual_minimum_addition_order: broad/high-breadth lessons with very low allocations
individual_maximum_reduction_stages: Stage 1: move remediation/repetition above each lesson base allocation to targeted homework
Stage 2: remove optional strategy blocks and reduce final review only to 1.00 hour; orientation remains 0.75 hour
Stage 3: compress guided practice in the strongest and best-covered lessons, never below 0.25 hour
Stage 4: remove any exact remaining amount from an eligible strongest lesson so actual live components do not exceed 35 hours
group_maximum_reduction_stages: Stage 1: remove group-size/diversity overhead above the highest individual lesson need
Stage 2: reduce optional group strategy/review time toward configured minima while preserving orientation
Stage 3: move student-specific need from the common core to transparent subgroup/individual support while preserving the 0.25-hour lesson minimum
Stage 4: remove any exact remaining amount from an eligible core block so actual components do not exceed the group maximum
course_constraint_allocation.individual_reduction_floor: Every lesson, broad or otherwise, retains at least 0.25 live hour. Orientation remains 0.75 hour; final review retains at least 1.00 hour.
course_constraint_allocation.group_reduction_floor: Every core group lesson retains at least 0.25 hour. Any student-specific need removed from common core is recorded as supplemental support.
course_constraint_allocation.removed_live_time_policy: Removed live lesson time is recorded and added to targeted homework/independent practice. The final component sum is recomputed; no total-only clamp is permitted.
course_constraint_allocation.infeasible_maximum_policy: If a future maximum is below mandatory minima, calculation fails with a configuration error.

base_hour_method.description: The three 1-3 profile scores remain visible for audit, while the approved lesson-specific calibrated value governs live base time.

homework_by_priority.rounding: half_up_to_nearest_0.25 after adding any transferred live time

foundational_override_rule.condition: Apply the max formula only when foundational_level is moderate or major. When foundational_level is none, core hours equal calibrated base times final multiplier.

shared_group_planning.description: A shared group profile pools correct and administered counts. Individual-equivalent lesson and strategy time is multiplied by 2.0 before existing group caps, balancing, redistribution, and whole-hour category rounding. The multiplier never depends on roster size.
